children.name: Castor
children.description: Beavers — the largest rodents in the Northern Hemisphere and one of the most transformative animals on Earth. After humans, beavers are the animal with the greatest impact on landscapes: their dams create ponds, wetlands, and floodplain meadows that support extraordinary biodiversity. A single beaver dam can raise the local water table, recharge aquifers, and reduce downstream flood risk. The lodge is constructed from branches plastered with mud and has an underwater entrance that keeps it secure from most predators. Beaver pairs mate for life and live in family groups with young from multiple years. Both Eurasian and North American beavers were hunted nearly to extinction for their dense, waterproof fur and for castoreum — a musk-gland secretion once used in medicine and perfumery.
